rationale: The citing paper is a conceptual editorial on how to write “sticky” JMR articles and explicitly notes it uses papers from the June 2008 issue as illustrations, rather than building on their substantive findings. The cited paper is an empirical donation/identity-congruency study with a different topical focus, and the matched evidence is only a single DOI reference with no contextual quote indicating methodological or theoretical dependence. This looks like an illustrative same-issue mention rather than substantive use.
